title_en: Zakat, Fasting, and Hajj in Al-Mudawwanah
title_ar: Zakat, Fasting, and Hajj in Al-Mudawwanah
content_en:
The chapters on zakat, sawm, and hajj in Al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra record Imam Malik's positions on the financial and ritual pillars of Islam as transmitted by Ibn al-Qasim. The question-and-answer format reveals how early Maliki scholars worked through specific cases and edge questions in applying the general principles of the worship law.

On zakat, Al-Mudawwanah records the Maliki positions on each category of zakatable wealth with the specific details that characterized Malik's approach. On the question of combining silver and gold for the nisab, Ibn al-Qasim records that Malik required them to be combined: if a person has some gold and some silver that together reach the equivalent of the nisab in either metal, zakat is due. This position — opposite to the Shafi'i position of separate assessment — reflects the Maliki view that gold and silver serve the same monetary function and should be treated as a single category of monetary wealth.

For zakat on agricultural produce, the Maliki school applies the obligation to a wider range of crops than some other schools. Malik's position, as recorded in Al-Mudawwanah, is that zakat is due on every crop that people cultivate for food and that can be dried and stored. This includes not only the major grains (wheat, barley, dates) but a range of additional crops cultivated in the Mediterranean and North African agricultural context. The questions recorded in Al-Mudawwanah reflect the specific crops of Medina and its surrounding region.

The fasting chapters record the Maliki positions on the intention for Ramadan fasting, the nullifiers, and the expiation. The Maliki school is notable for requiring the kaffarah for any deliberate nullification of the Ramadan fast — not only for intercourse but also for deliberate eating or drinking without a valid excuse. This stricter position, extending the kaffarah beyond the specific case mentioned in the hadith by analogy, reflects the Maliki school's willingness to extend legal rulings through reasoning from the underlying principle (maslahah and sadd al-dhara'i').

On hajj, Al-Mudawwanah records the Maliki positions on the types of ihram, the miqat boundaries, and the rites. Malik's strong preference for the tamattu' form — reflected in his students' transmissions — and his teachings on the proper performance of the pilgrimage rites are preserved in the question-and-answer format. Questions about pilgrims from North Africa, who faced a much longer journey than Medinan pilgrims, are addressed with the awareness of this geographic reality.

The chapters on zakat al-fitr record Malik's insistence on giving food rather than cash, a position that places the Maliki school in contrast with some contemporary practices that allow cash payment. Al-Mudawwanah records Ibn al-Qasim's explanation of the basis for this position: the prophetic command specifies food, and the substitution of cash for food, however practically convenient, was not the practice Malik observed in Medina.
title_id: Zakat, Puasa, dan Haji dalam Al-Mudawwanah
content_id:
Bab-bab tentang zakat, shaum, dan haji dalam Al-Mudawwanah al-Kubra merekam posisi-posisi Imam Malik tentang pilar-pilar keuangan dan ritual Islam sebagaimana ditransmisikan oleh Ibn al-Qasim. Format tanya-jawab mengungkapkan bagaimana para ulama Maliki awal bekerja melalui pertanyaan-pertanyaan praktis yang dihadapi oleh masyarakat Muslim di Madinah, Afrika Utara, dan berbagai wilayah lain di bawah kekhalifahan Abbasiyah awal.

Dalam bidang zakat, Imam Malik dikenal dengan beberapa posisi khas. Beliau mewajibkan zakat atas perhiasan emas dan perak yang digunakan sehari-hari, sebuah posisi yang berbeda dari mazhab Hanafi. Dalam zakat pertanian, beliau mengikuti pembedaan antara tanaman yang diairi dengan hujan (dikenai 10%) dan tanaman yang diairi dengan tenaga tambahan (dikenai 5%). Pembahasan tentang zakat ternak sangat rinci karena Madinah dan wilayah Arabia secara tradisional merupakan masyarakat yang bergantung pada peternakan.

Dalam bidang puasa, Al-Mudawwanah mendokumentasikan pendekatan Imam Malik yang sangat ketat tentang kafarat bagi yang membatalkan puasa Ramadan dengan sengaja — beliau mensyaratkan kafarat bahkan dalam kondisi di mana mazhab lain mungkin hanya mewajibkan qadha. Pandangan Malik tentang puasa qadha' dan apakah boleh menundanya juga dibahas dengan rinci.

Dalam bidang haji, transmisi Ibn al-Qasim dari Malik mencakup seluruh manasik haji dengan penekanan pada praktik-praktik yang beliau saksikan langsung di Makkah dan Madinah. Pendekatan Malik yang sangat mengandalkan "amalan penduduk Madinah" sebagai sumber hukum sangat terlihat dalam pembahasan-pembahasan haji, di mana beliau sering merujuk pada apa yang biasa dilakukan oleh masyarakat Madinah sebagai bukti sunnah yang hidup.
